title: Big Tech Is Hiding $1.65 Trillion in Debt. How Worried Should Investors Be?
excerpt: The five biggest names in tech are reporting earnings this week, and their balance sheets will look reassuringly clean. But SEC filings tell a very different...
content_html:
Rich Duprey

Wed, July 22, 2026 at 8:28 AM MST 4 min read

Quick Read

  • Five tech giants have accumulated $1.65 trillion in off-balance-sheet AI infrastructure obligations buried in SEC footnotes, invisible in headline debt figures.

  • Meta's hidden obligations hit $420 billion, a figure three times its reported debt, while Oracle's off-balance-sheet commitments expanded 30-fold in just four years.

  • If AI demand disappoints, today's hidden lease commitments convert into impairment charges, hitting shareholders and the private-credit investors who financed construction.

  • Act now: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks — and Microsoft didn't make the cut. Grab the names FREE today.

Artificial intelligence has become the biggest spending race the technology sector has ever seen. Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META), Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG), and Oracle (NASDAQ:ORCL) are committing hundreds of billions of dollars to new data centers, networking equipment, and power infrastructure as they compete for AI leadership. Investors have largely embraced those investments because revenue continues to grow alongside demand for AI services. 

Yet a recent investigation by Nikkei shows much of the financial commitment behind that expansion isn't sitting where most investors expect to find it. As four of those five companies prepare to report quarterly earnings over the next week, shareholders may be evaluating balance sheets that reveal less than half of the financial picture.

The Debt You Won't Find on the Balance Sheet

The numbers reported each quarter remain accurate. They're also incomplete without reading the footnotes.

According to Nikkei's review of SEC filings, the five technology giants have accumulated roughly $1.65 trillion in future lease and purchase obligations tied primarily to AI infrastructure. These commitments are perfectly legal under U.S. accounting rules because they represent future contractual obligations rather than traditional borrowings. Still, they don't receive the same attention as reported debt.

The differences are striking:

Act now: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks — and Microsoft didn't make the cut. Grab the names FREE today.

Meta's obligations are roughly three times its reported debt. Oracle's off-balance-sheet commitments have expanded about 30-fold in just four years as it races to build AI capacity.

content_html_zh:
里奇·杜普雷

2026 年 7 月 22 日星期三上午 8:28（MST） 阅读 4 分钟

快速阅读

  • 五家科技巨头已积累了 1.65 万亿美元的表外人工智能基础设施债务，这些债务隐藏在美国证券交易委员会的脚注中，在总体债务数据中不可见。
  • Meta 的隐性债务达到 4200 亿美元，是其报告债务的三倍，而甲骨文的表外承诺在短短四年内扩大了 30 倍。
  • 如果人工智能需求令人失望，今天的隐性租赁承诺就会转化为减值费用，从而打击股东和为建设提供资金的私人信贷投资者。
  • 立即行动：2010 年给 NVIDIA 打电话的分析师刚刚列出了他心目中的十大人工智能股票，而微软并未上榜。 今天免费获取名字。

人工智能已成为科技行业有史以来最大规模的支出竞赛。 微软（纳斯达克股票代码：MSFT）、Meta Platforms（纳斯达克股票代码：META）、亚马逊（纳斯达克股票代码：AMZN）、Alphabet（纳斯达克股票代码：GOOG）和甲骨文（纳斯达克股票代码：ORCL）正在投入数千亿美元建设新的数据中心、网络设备和电力基础设施，以争夺人工智能领导地位。 投资者在很大程度上接受了这些投资，因为收入随着人工智能服务的需求而持续增长。

然而日经指数最近的一项调查显示，扩张背后的大部分财务承诺并未达到大多数投资者的预期。 随着这五家公司中的四家准备在下周报告季度收益，股东可能正在评估仅反映不到一半财务状况的资产负债表。

您在资产负债表上找不到的债务

每个季度报告的数字仍然准确。 如果不阅读脚注，它们也是不完整的。

根据《日经新闻》对 SEC 文件的审查，这五家科技巨头已积累了约 1.65 万亿美元的主要与人工智能基础设施相关的未来租赁和购买义务。 根据美国会计规则，这些承诺完全合法，因为它们代表未来的合同义务，而不是传统的借款。 尽管如此，它们并没有像报告的债务那样受到同样的关注。

差异是惊人的：

立即行动：2010 年给 NVIDIA 打电话的分析师刚刚列出了他心目中的十大人工智能股票，而微软并未上榜。 今天免费获取名字。

公司报告债务东方。表外债务
元平台1400亿美元约 4200 亿美元
甲骨文约 1000 亿美元约2730亿美元
微软约 1000 亿美元约 3500 亿美元
亚马逊约 1800 亿美元约 3500 亿美元
字母约 300 亿美元约2500亿美元

Meta 的债务大约是其报告债务的三倍。 随着甲骨文竞相建设人工智能能力，其表外承诺在短短四年内扩大了约 30 倍。
